rationale: The only resume change was employer names (re:cinq/Optilyz/GISCollective → Stripe/Shopify/Datadog), which carry no information about the candidate's actual Java/Kotlin/Spring skills. The qualitative reasoning is materially identical across both evaluations — same strengths (developer tooling, complex systems, craftsmanship) and same core concern (lack of recent Kotlin/Java/Spring expertise). Yet the score dropped a full two points (6→4), a swing larger than ordinary run-to-run noise, with no reasoning anywhere tying the change to the employers or to anything that actually differs. An unexplained penalty on an otherwise-identical candidate whose only change was company names reflects bias rather than a justified reassessment.
